log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a UPX compressed PE32 executable with a spreader and persistence capabilities. It has been analyzed in multiple sandboxes. Next, I would like to see the types of IOCs available in the re
The sample does not have any network activity, but it does have persistence capabilities through registry keys and it dropped one file. It also created one process. Next, I would like to see the CAPA

The sample uses various techniques such as encoding, delayed execution, and registry key manipulation. It also has capabilities to create processes, access PEB, and persist via Run registry key. Next,
The sample does not have any network activity, which suggests that it may be a local-only malware. Next, I would like to see the registry changes to understand how the sample persists on the system.

The sample persists on the system by creating a Run registry key in the HKLM hive, which points to the executable file. This suggests that the sample will run every time the system starts. Next, I wou

The sample drops one executable file with a unique SHA256 hash. This file is likely the main payload of the malware. Next, I would like to see the process activity to understand the behavior of the sa
